forecast-period.text: A high pressure system over the Tasman sea extends a weak ridge over SA. A trough over central WA extends over southwest SA, and is forecast to move across the west and south later Tuesday, pushing into the northeast of SA on Wednesday. Another high pressure system is expected to become established south of the Bight late in the week.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Northerly 15 to 20 knots turning westerly in the late evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
Mostly sunny day. 60% chance of showers in the evening.
Westerly 10 to 15 knots turning southerly 15 to 20 knots during the morning and early afternoon.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres north of Cowell to Wallaroo during the afternoon.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
The chance of a thunderstorm north of Cowell to Wallaroo in the morning. Mostly sunny afternoon.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots becoming variable about 10 knots during the morning then becoming southerly 15 to 25 knots during the day.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the evening.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Port Neill to Port Victoria.
